paper_overview.citation: Jaiswal, M., Hussain, J., Gupta, S.K. et al. Comprehensive evaluation of water quality status for entire stretch of Yamuna River, India. Environ Monit Assess 191, 208 (2019). https://doi.org/10.1007/s10661-019-7312-8
paper_overview.study_region: Yamuna River, India, covering seven states including National Capital Territory (NCT) of Delhi

data_sources.description: Water quality stations along with their latitude and longitude data and catchment areas

data_sources.location: Supplementary file; Tables S2 and S3
data_sources.description: Concentrations of water quality parameters at monitoring stations during monsoon and non-monsoon
Indian Standards classes for water usage
Indian Standards (IS 1982, IS 1991) for water quality parameters
Correlation matrix among water qualities parameters during monsoon season
Correlation matrix among water qualities parameters during non-monsoon season
Loadings of the significant principal components for water quality parameters
Layout of the Yamuna River under investigation
Water quality index (WQI) at each monitoring station along the Yamuna River
Standardization of physicochemical parameters to zero mean and unit variance
Loading plot of water quality parameters in PCA
DO profile along the Yamuna River and corresponding oxygen sag curve

sites.description: Paonta, an upstream monitoring station on the Yamuna River
sites.source_quote: The monitoring stations used to cover the entire Yamuna stretch were Paonta, Kalanaur, Mawi, Palla, Delhi, Mohana, Mathura, Agra, Etawah, Auraiya, Hamirpur, and Pratappur.
sites.source_location: Page 3, 'Sampling sites and sample collection' section

extraction_notes: Latitude and longitude for the sampling sites are mentioned to be in a supplementary file (Table S1), which was not accessible for extraction. The unit for Temperature (°C) is inferred from the text, not explicitly stated in Table 1. The unit for Electrical Conductivity (EC) is reported as 'µ/cm' in Table 1 but 'µS/cm' in the text (Page 7); 'µS/cm' was chosen for standardization. Units for Total Alkalinity, Total Hardness, Calcium Hardness, and Magnesium Hardness are specified 'as CaCO3' in the footnote of Table 1.
